activities.markdown:
# The Pocket: Where Great Grooves Live

'Pocket' is the term musicians use to describe that indescribable quality of a groove that feels deeply locked in — not just on the beat, but in the precise rhythmic center of the music.

**What pocket actually is:**
Pocket is about the relationship between your groove and the quarter-note pulse. Notes can be:
- **On the beat** (directly on the metronome click)
- **Slightly ahead** (rushing — creates tension, urgency, excitement)
- **Slightly behind** (dragging — creates weight, heaviness, laid-back feel)

A drummer who plays consistently in one of these positions has their own 'pocket.' The issue is inconsistency — some notes ahead, some behind, some on the click. This creates an unsettled, uncomfortable groove.

**Groove styles and pocket position:**
- **New Orleans/second line**: typically slightly ahead — urgent, celebratory
- **Hip-hop inspired R&B**: behind the beat — heavy, inevitable
- **Dance/pop funk**: on the click or very slightly behind — predictable, comfortable

**Developing pocket:** Record yourself to a click. Listen back critically. Your instinctive relationship to the click is your natural groove tendency. Work to maintain that tendency consistently.

activities.title: Deep Pocket Funk Beat
activities.description: Medium tempo funk with ghost notes and syncopated kick. Play this with a slight laid-back feel — every note settling just slightly after the 16th-note grid position. The pocket comes from consistency of this subtle behind-the-beat placement.

activities.question: What does it mean for a drummer to play 'behind the beat'?
choices: Placing notes slightly after the strict grid position — creating a heavy, laid-back feel
Only using the back half of the drum kit
activities.explanation: Playing 'behind the beat' means placing notes slightly after the strict metronome position — not rushing, not dragging in tempo, but consistently placing the feel just slightly heavy. This creates the laid-back, inevitable quality found in hip-hop-influenced R&B and deep soul music.
